processing.reason: Spike noise is detected (cosmic rays/artifacts), which median filtering is uniquely effective at removing. Despite sharp peaks and high peak density favoring Savitzky-Golay, spike noise must be addressed first as it corrupts peak shape more severely than moderate Gaussian noise. A small kernel_size of 5 preserves sharp peak shapes and resolves closely spaced peaks while effectively eliminating isolated spike artifacts. The moderate noise level (SNR ~36) does not require aggressive smoothing, and the narrow median window minimally distorts the 22 detected sharp peaks. A subsequent Savitzky-Golay pass could follow if needed, but the median filter alone should suffice given the moderate noise.
